skill.name: chatbot-implementation
skill.description: Documents a RAG chatbot design with API flow, vector search, UI behavior, styling, and personalization notes.
skill.summary: Guide implementation of a textbook-focused RAG chatbot.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and dismissed as false positives. Inline Markdown code was mistaken for shell execution, and prose substrings were mistaken for weak cryptography. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, malware behavior, or executable code was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged inline Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution. The locations contain file paths, field names, route names, and CSS variable text, not runnable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is Markdown documentation only. No executable shell, Ruby, script block, or user-controlled command path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on prose lines. The matched lines describe chatbot details and mobile responsive design, with no cryptographic API or algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence contains ordinary English words and does not reference DES, MD5, SHA1, or cryptographic functions. No code exists in the skill file.

content.value_statement: Teams often need a clear plan for connecting textbook content to conversational answers. This skill outlines the backend flow, vector search, UI behavior, and styling needs for a RAG chatbot.
actual_capabilities: Explains the API route that receives user queries and chat history.
Describes embedding flow with Gemini or OpenAI embedding models.
Maps vector search against a Qdrant textbook chunk collection.
Identifies expected chatbot UI features and responsive behavior.
Notes markdown rendering, context selection, and auth-aware answers.
limitations: Provides implementation guidance, not complete runnable source code.
Does not define exact environment variables or deployment settings.
Does not include database migration or ingestion scripts.
Requires separate validation for privacy, access control, and model costs.

use_cases.title: Plan a Course Chatbot
use_cases.description: Use the skill to outline how textbook chunks become cited answers in a learning product.

use_cases.title: Implement Backend Retrieval
use_cases.description: Use the backend flow to connect query embedding, Qdrant search, context assembly, and model response generation.

use_cases.title: Review Chat UI Scope
use_cases.description: Use the UI checklist to confirm chat window behavior, sizing controls, markdown rendering, and mobile layout.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the RAG chatbot architecture from this skill in simple steps. Include backend, vector search, and UI responsibilities.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Implementation Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this chatbot skill into a development checklist for a Next.js project using Qdrant and a hosted language model.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Chatbot Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare my planned textbook chatbot against this skill. Identify missing backend, retrieval, UI, and styling requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Define Technical Acceptance Criteria
prompt_templates.prompt: Create acceptance criteria for implementing this RAG chatbot. Cover query handling, retrieval quality, response generation, UI states, and mobile behavior.

output_examples.input: Plan a RAG chatbot for textbook content.
output_examples.output: A structured plan covering query intake, embedding generation, Qdrant retrieval, context assembly, model response generation, and responsive chat UI behavior.

output_examples.input: Check whether my chatbot scope is complete.
output: Backend route handles query and history.
Vector search returns relevant textbook chunks.
Responses render markdown and respect selected context.
The floating chat window works across screen sizes.

output_examples.input: Write acceptance criteria for the chat UI.
output_examples.output: A concise criteria list for floating window behavior, size controls, markdown rendering, selected-text context, auth-aware personalization, and mobile responsiveness.

best_practices: Validate user access before using profile data in personalized answers.
Log retrieval quality and failed searches without storing sensitive chat content.
Test mobile layout, markdown rendering, and selected-text context together.
anti_patterns: Do not send entire textbooks to the model when vector retrieval is available.
Do not personalize answers with profile data unless the user is authorized.
Do not treat this skill as a complete production security review.

faq.question: Does this skill include runnable chatbot code?
faq.answer: No. It documents the intended chatbot architecture, backend flow, vector search behavior, UI scope, and styling requirements.

faq.question: Which vector database does it describe?
faq.answer: It describes Qdrant as the vector database for storing and searching textbook embeddings.

faq.question: Which model providers are mentioned?
faq.answer: The skill mentions Gemini or OpenAI for embeddings, and Gemini Flash or Pro for response generation.

faq.question: Can it help with frontend planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists expected chat UI features, including a floating window, size controls, markdown rendering, and mobile responsiveness.

faq.question: Does it handle textbook ingestion?
faq.answer: No. It assumes textbook chunks and embeddings already exist in a Qdrant collection.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is documentation only, and the static security findings were false positives.
